---
title: "One-Scene Smoke Checks"
phase: thesis
audience: developer
status: current
owner: code
format: html
---

# One-Scene Smoke Checks {#one-scene-smoke-checks}

This page defines the smallest real-data smoke path for ARIA-NBV: build one
immutable VIN offline-store sample, inspect it in Rerun, and run VIN diagnostics
against the configured offline source. Use it before generating larger oracle
stores or trusting a training run.

For environment setup, dataset placement, and checkpoint paths, start from
[`SETUP.md`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/ARIA-NBV/blob/main/SETUP.md)
or the public [setup page](../setup.qmd).

## What This Smoke Covers {#what-this-smoke-covers}

The real one-scene path checks these contracts together:

- ASE/ATEK shard discovery and GT mesh loading;
- EVL backbone checkpoint resolution;
- candidate sampling and validity metadata;
- PyTorch3D candidate depth rendering;
- oracle RRI labels and metric vectors;
- immutable offline-store manifest, sample index, split arrays, and shards;
- Rerun geometry logging for semidense points, reference pose, candidate
  frusta, mesh, OBBs, and stored metrics;
- VIN one-batch summary through the training-facing offline source.

It does not prove full-store coverage, final thesis split quality, rollout data
quality, or VIN training convergence.

## Preconditions {#preconditions}

Run from a synced `aria_nbv/` environment with at least one ASE EFM shard, its
GT mesh, and the EVL checkpoint available:

```text
.data/ase_efm/<scene_id>/shards-*.tar
.data/ase_meshes/scene_ply_<scene_id>.ply
.logs/ckpts/model_lite.pth
```

The sidecar config `.configs/build_vin_offline_rerun_smoke_v6.toml` currently
sets `max_samples = 1`, `samples_per_shard = 1`, `overwrite = true`, and
`store_dir = "vin_offline_rerun_smoke_v6"`. It is intentionally separate from
the default `vin_offline` training store.

## Step 1: Validate the Writer Config {#step-1-validate-the-writer-config}

```sh
cd aria_nbv
uv run nbv-build-offline --config-path ../.configs/build_vin_offline_rerun_smoke_v6.toml --dry-run
```

The dry run should resolve the store under
`.data/offline_cache/vin_offline_rerun_smoke_v6/` without loading snippets,
running EVL, or writing shards.

## Step 2: Build One Offline Sample {#step-2-build-one-offline-sample}

```sh
cd aria_nbv
uv run nbv-build-offline --config-path ../.configs/build_vin_offline_rerun_smoke_v6.toml
```

Expected files:

```text
.data/offline_cache/vin_offline_rerun_smoke_v6/manifest.json
.data/offline_cache/vin_offline_rerun_smoke_v6/sample_index.jsonl
.data/offline_cache/vin_offline_rerun_smoke_v6/splits/all.npy
.data/offline_cache/vin_offline_rerun_smoke_v6/splits/val.npy
.data/offline_cache/vin_offline_rerun_smoke_v6/shards/
```

The manifest should report one sample, one shard, and `interrupted = false`.
If this step fails on a CPU-only machine, treat that as an environment/resource
limit unless the same config also fails on a CUDA workstation.

## Step 3: Save a Rerun Recording {#step-3-save-a-rerun-recording}

```sh
cd aria_nbv
uv run nbv-rerun-inspect --config-path ../.configs/rerun_offline_smoke_v6.toml --split val --index 0 --save ../.artifacts/rerun/offline_smoke_v6.rrd
```

The Rerun recording should show the reference camera, candidate frusta,
semidense reconstruction points, GT mesh or OBB layers when present, and
candidate metadata aligned with stored RRI values. The inspector is
diagnostic-only: display downsampling must not mutate labels, rankings, cached
geometry, or model inputs.

Open the saved recording in the native viewer when available:

```sh
cd aria_nbv
uv run nbv-rerun-inspect --config-path ../.configs/rerun_offline_smoke_v6.toml --split val --index 0 --view
```

For browser-based review on a local machine:

```sh
cd aria_nbv
uv run nbv-rerun-inspect --config-path ../.configs/rerun_offline_smoke_v6.toml --split val --index 0 --serve-web --web-viewer-port 9877
```

## Step 4: Run VIN Diagnostics {#step-4-run-vin-diagnostics}

Validate the default training-facing offline source:

```sh
cd aria_nbv
uv run nbv-summary --config-path offline_only.toml
```

This command reads `.configs/offline_only.toml`, forces summary mode, disables
W&B, uses one batch, and prints the VIN summary for a real oracle batch. Use it
to catch missing store files, split issues, tensor-shape drift, checkpoint
resolution errors, and obvious candidate/RRI mismatches before training.

The default `vin_offline` store can be a partial diagnostic store. Confirm the
manifest and sample index before using it for thesis figures or training
claims.

## Step 5: Optional Synthetic Rollout Trace {#step-5-optional-synthetic-rollout-trace}

The rollout trace smoke is CPU-only and does not require ASE data:

```sh
cd aria_nbv
uv run nbv-rollout-trace-smoke --output-path ../.artifacts/rollouts/synthetic_rollout_traces.msgpack
```

Use it to verify trace serialization and replay plumbing. It is not an oracle
RRI, Rerun, VIN, or ASE data smoke substitute.

## Pass Criteria {#pass-criteria}

The smoke passes when:

- the writer dry run resolves the expected sidecar store path;
- the one-sample build writes a non-interrupted manifest and split files;
- the Rerun command saves an `.rrd` under `.artifacts/rerun/`;
- the Rerun view shows plausible frame alignment rather than hidden or inverted
  geometry;
- `nbv-summary --config-path offline_only.toml` yields one real oracle batch
  summary without entering training mode.

Failures should be fixed at the data, config, geometry, or inspector contract
that produced them. Do not hide them by changing visualization-only
downsampling or by silently switching to an online dataset.

## Docs Sanity {#docs-sanity}

After changing setup or smoke documentation:

```sh
make qmd-frontmatter-check
cd docs
quarto render contents/setup.qmd
quarto render contents/impl/one_scene_smoke.qmd
quarto render contents/impl/rerun_offline_inspector.qmd
quarto check
```
